paragraphs.body: Main Point: The Smithfield Street Bridge does not look like what I expected, but it is beautiful and unique.

Exposition: In fact, I heard about Pittsburgh back in high school in a social studies class. We were having a lecture about industrialization, and my social study teacher set Pittsburgh as an example. I don’t know anywhere, specifically Pittsburgh. It was not a big city like New York, but it had very good universities. My first impression about Pittsburgh was that it was just a place like the northeaster cities of China. I am from Harbin, which is a northeastern city in Heilongjiang Province, China. Harbin is more of an agricultural city, but it was very industrial back then.

Similarities between Harbin and Pittsburgh -Both have bridges -Both have a famous river -Both industrialized Then I realized that Harbin had a lot of bridges connecting the lands across the rivers. It was just like what Pittsburgh was known for – for having so many bridges. Smithfield Street Bridge is probably the most famous bridge in Pittsburg. Just by hearing its name, I expect this bridge to have a long history cause it sounds like it’s named after some famous person. Conflict: After coming to Pittsburgh, I realized that the bridges here is shorter than the ones I see in Harbin. The river here is narrower than the Songhua River I saw in Harbin, so the bridges don’t have to be that long. There weren’t any details that surprised me.

Rising Action: Similarities: -Both Yangmingtan Bridge and Smithfield Street Bridge get its name from the street it crosses. -Both very important for the city -Both connect different parts of the city that were segregated by the river → This allows the people in the city to go across the river, like the nervous system in one’s body that allows one’s organs and brains to communicate. Differences: -Yangmingtan Bridge is way longer than the Smithfield Street Bridge. -The bridges in Pittsburgh are closer to each other, unlike the ones in Harbin that are far away from each other.

Climax: I don’t think my comparisons have much to do with languages. I do see the different strategies for building bridges between the 2 cities. Harbin usually just builds one major bridge and makes it wide enough for a lot of people to go through. Pittsburgh, on the other hand, built many bridges so people from each part of the city have easy access to their closest bridge.

Resolution: I compared the bridges in Pittsburgh with our nervous system. It is so important to a city that bridges are more than just structures. They reflect the personality of a city and people’s wisdom.
